§3.3 Transpose

Definition 3.3.1.

The transpose of a matrix, , is , such that .

Then, we have the following rules for transpose

  1. Addition

  2. multiplication

    The reverse of and is due to is ‘s -th row dot ‘s -th column, which is ‘s -th row dot ‘s i-th column.

  3. Inverse

    It suffices to show

    Therefore, the dot product, also as known as the inner product of two vectors, and is .

Since vectors could be viewed as special cases of matrices, transpose is also a pertinent operation regarding vectors.

Definition 3.3.2.

Fix vectors and , with dimensions and , respectively. In the case where , define the inner product between and to be

which is exactly the dot product between and . By transposing instead, define

producing a matrix.

Interestingly, we have

Definition 3.3.3.

A matrix is symmetric iff .

We have the following properties of symmetric matrices.

  1. If matrix is invertible and symmetric, then is symmetric.

  2. is symmetric

  3. In elimination, , introduce the diagonal matrix such that and both have all ‘s on their diagonals, . In this way, storage and amount of computation for solving a symmetric linear system are both cut in half.
